steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to show that the same sound (snoring) is written and pronounced very differently across languages, using both specific examples: Batak 'korrrr' and Mandarin 'hu lu.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Though they both represent the sound of snoring, ... are written and pronounced very differently': explicitly pairs the shared sound with both examples and names the contrast in written and spoken form. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Onomatopoeias ... are often written and pronounced very differently from language to language': makes a general claim without using either specific example. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The Batak word "korrrr" and the Mandarin Chinese word "hu lu" are onomatopoeias that represent the sound of snoring': includes both examples and the shared sound but omits the contrast (written/pronounced differently). ✗
  • Choice D — 'The Batak word "korrrr," ... is an onomatopoeia': mentions only one example and no contrast. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
